title: Developer Restructuring Loses Momentum
text:
Developer Restructuring Loses Momentum

Xiaoxi Zhang

Gavekal Dragonomics | 29 Jun 2026

China’s property bust is now a full five years old: it began in mid-2021 when the financial problems of China Evergrande Group started to snowball, delivering a blow to market confidence and eventually leading many private-sector developers into formal default. Those troubled developers have struggled ever since to move on from the crisis. Out of the 47 developers that have defaulted on publicly traded bonds, only 17 have successfully completed either an onshore or offshore debt restructuring.Progress seemed to be accelerating in 2025, when 10 developers finalized restructuring deals, including some big names like Country Garden and Shimao. But the pace has slowed again in 2026: with the year half over, only three developers have completed restructuring agreements: Powerlong Real Estate, Fantasia Holdings, and China SCE Group. The government’s support for private-sector developers, never very strong, has weakened even further this year. The momentum for debt restructuring is fading as officials decide, for some good reasons, that there is no longer much upside in helping developers.
